body:
## Rationale

MetalForge's Genre Gear Guide section has high commercial intent queries with strong affiliate potential. Current coverage:
- **Live guides (4 entries):** base set shipped with #1794
- **Batch 1 (#2914, ai-fix):** best-cymbals-for-death-metal + best-drum-pedals-for-black-metal + best-drum-kits-for-thrash-metal
- **Batch 2 (#2952, seo-proposal):** best-snare-drums-for-metal + best-cymbals-for-progressive-metal + best-drum-kits-for-djent

**Batch 3 (this proposal)** targets three different gear *categories* not yet addressed as standalone guides:

1. **best-hi-hats-for-metal** — The existing cymbals guide mentions hi-hats as a sub-section, but a standalone guide captures 'best metal hi-hats', 'hi-hat recommendations metal', 'which hi-hats do metal drummers use?' as primary queries. Lars Ulrich, Joey Jordison, Mike Mangini, Matt Greiner all have documented hi-hat preferences in DB.

2. **best-bass-drum-pedals-for-metal** — Distinct from 'best drum pedals for black metal' (batch 1, which is genre+gear specific). This is the generic 'what bass drum pedal should I buy for metal?' query cluster — Iron Cobra, Demon Drive, DW 9000, Trick Bigfoot all feature in MetalForge drummer profiles.

3. **best-drum-heads-for-metal** — High commercial intent (consumable, frequent replacement), zero current MetalForge coverage. Evans EMAD, Remo Powerstroke, Aquarian Super Kick — all documented in existing drummer profiles. Connects to 'how to get a metal drum sound' queries.

**Dedup verified:** None of these three slugs appear in `packages/frontend/data/genreGearGuides.js`, and none are in batches 1-2. They cover different gear categories (hi-hats vs cymbals, generic pedals vs genre-specific pedals, heads = entirely new category).

## Files to touch

- `packages/frontend/data/genreGearGuides.js` — add 3 new guide entries
- `public/llms/guides/best-hi-hats-for-metal.md` — new LLM markdown file
- `public/llms/guides/best-bass-drum-pedals-for-metal.md` — new LLM markdown file
- `public/llms/guides/best-drum-heads-for-metal.md` — new LLM markdown file

## Template / data shape

Follow exact pattern of existing Genre Gear Guides (see `genreGearGuides.js` structure). Each entry needs:
- `slug`: best-hi-hats-for-metal / best-bass-drum-pedals-for-metal / best-drum-heads-for-metal
- `title`, `metaTitle`, `metaDescription`
- `recommendations`: 4-6 product recommendations with pros/cons and price ranges
- `faq`: ≥3 Q&A pairs
- `relatedDrummers`: 3-5 drummer IDs from MetalForge roster who use the featured gear
- `schema`: ItemList + FAQPage

## Quality gate

- ≥300 words per guide (description + context + recommendations)
- FAQ ≥3 Q&A pairs per guide
- ≥3 drummer profile internal links per guide (e.g., Lars Ulrich uses Zildjian hi-hats, Tomas Haake uses Iron Cobra pedals)
- ItemList schema listing each recommendation
- FAQPage schema for the Q&A section
- 3 LLM markdown files at `public/llms/guides/`

## Estimated organic uplift

Generic metal gear queries ('best hi-hats for metal', 'best drum pedals for metal') are high-volume commercial searches with strong affiliate revenue potential. Unlike genre-specific guides, these capture the broadest metal audience regardless of sub-genre preference. Drum heads specifically have repeat-purchase intent — strong affiliate conversion.

## Verify

1. 3 new slugs appear in genreGearGuides.js
2. Each guide page renders at /guides/best-hi-hats-for-metal, /guides/best-bass-drum-pedals-for-metal, /guides/best-drum-heads-for-metal
3. Each page contains ≥3 product recommendations with drummer cross-references
4. All 3 LLM markdown files created at public/llms/guides/
5. Sitemap includes the 3 new guide URLs (auto-synced from GENRE_GEAR_GUIDES module per #1794)

## Done

- [x] 3 guide entries added to genreGearGuides.js
- [x] 3 LLM markdown files created
- [x] Internal drummer links included in each guide
title: SEO batch: Genre Gear Guide batch 3 — best-hi-hats-for-metal + best-bass-drum-pedals-for-metal + best-drum-heads-for-metal (~3 guides + 3 LLM files)
validationNotes: Re-verified 2026-06-29: all 3 guides present in genreGearGuides.js (best-drum-heads-for-metal line 473, best-hi-hats-for-metal line 4707, best-bass-drum-pedals-for-metal line 5208); 3 LLM markdown files at public/llms/guides/ (1500+ words each, 9-10 drummer links each, 5 FAQ entries each); genreGearGuides.js passes node --check. Originally shipped across commits 054f6fa2, 707c8b6b.
